overview: The Doxygen Filter extracts [Doxygen](http://www.stack.nl/~dimitri/doxygen/)-style comments from source code for translation. It supports C++-style (`///`), Javadoc-style (`/**`), Qt-style (`/*!`), and Python-style (`'''` or `"""`) comment blocks. Doxygen special commands, HTML commands, and XML commands are recognized and converted to inline codes. The filter preserves line numbers so that source and translated line numbers maintain a one-to-one correspondence.

custom_commands.help:
Define custom commands using regex patterns to match non-zero-width runs of text within comments. Matches are converted into inline codes.

Each entry supports:
- `pattern` — a regex matching text within the comment
- `type` — `PLACEHOLDER`, `OPENING`, or `CLOSING`
- `inline` — `true` for inline, `false` for block-level (default: `false`)
- `pair` — paired command name (for `OPENING`/`CLOSING` types)
- `translatable` — whether content is translatable (default: `true`)
- `parameters` — ordered list of command parameters

All options from `doxygen_commands` entries are supported except `COMMAND_NAME`.
examples: `pattern: "###ACCESS_CHECKS###.*?;"` — matches custom placeholder markers ending with semicolons

doxygen_commands.help:
Defines how known Doxygen special commands are handled during extraction. Pre-populated with the full set of [Doxygen special commands](http://www.stack.nl/~dimitri/doxygen/commands.html).

Each entry is keyed by command name (without prefix/suffix, case-sensitive) and supports:
- `type` — `PLACEHOLDER`, `OPENING`, or `CLOSING`
- `inline` — `true` for inline, `false` for block-level (default: `false`)
- `pair` — paired command name (e.g., `code` pairs with `endcode`)
- `translatable` — whether the block content is translatable (default: `true`)
- `parameters` — ordered list of parameter descriptors, each with:
  - `name` — organizational label (not used by filter)
  - `length` — `WORD`, `LINE`, `PHRASE`, or `PARAGRAPH`
  - `required` — affects how aggressively text is parsed as a parameter (default: `true`)
  - `translatable` — whether the parameter is translatable (default: `true`)
notes: The `parameters` listing is optional. When present, parameters must be listed in the order they appear after the command.
Parameters with non-whitespace delimiters (e.g. `.py` in `\code{.py}`) are **not** currently supported.
examples:
```yaml
doxygen_commands:
  code:
    type: OPENING
    inline: false
    pair: endcode
    translatable: false
```

```yaml
doxygen_commands:
  brief:
    type: PLACEHOLDER
    inline: true
    parameters:
      - name: description
        length: LINE
        required: true
        translatable: true
```
doxygen_commands.values: Each command entry is keyed by the command name as it appears in the Doxygen comment (e.g., `code` for `\code`). `type` must be one of `PLACEHOLDER`, `OPENING`, or `CLOSING`. Parameter `length` must be one of `WORD`, `LINE`, `PHRASE` (double-quote delimited string), or `PARAGRAPH`.

html_commands.help: Defines how HTML commands within Doxygen comments are handled. Pre-populated with the full set of [Doxygen HTML commands](http://www.stack.nl/~dimitri/doxygen/htmlcmds.html). Uses the same entry format as `doxygen_commands`.

preserve_whitespace.help: Prevents the filter from collapsing whitespace in extracted comment text. By default, the filter collapses single linebreaks within a text run, producing potentially very long single-line paragraphs. No effort is made to enforce a maximum line width on output.
notes: Without this option, each translatable paragraph is collapsed to a single (potentially very long) line.

limitations: Single linebreaks in a text run that are not part of a Doxygen command are collapsed into a single long line. No maximum line width is enforced on output.
Command parameters with non-whitespace delimiters (e.g. `.py` in `\code{.py}`) are not supported.
Non-translatable command parameters are not exposed for any special processing.
processingNotes: **Encoding detection**: Uses Unicode BOM if present (UTF-8, UTF-16, etc.); otherwise falls back to the default encoding specified when opening the document.
**Inline codes**: The full set of Doxygen special commands, HTML commands, and XML commands are recognized and converted to inline codes in extracted text units.
**Line number preservation**: The filter maintains a one-to-one correspondence between source and translated line numbers.

examples.title: Extracting Doxygen comments
examples.description: Shows how various Doxygen comment styles are recognized and extracted for translation.

examples.title: Inline code conversion from commands
examples.description: Doxygen commands like `\class` and `\brief` are converted to inline codes, while translatable text is preserved as text units.
examples.input:
```cpp
/*! \class Test class.h "inc/class.h"
 *  \brief This is a test class.
 *
 * Some details about the Test class
 */
```
examples.output:
```
Text Unit 1: <1/><2/> This is a test class.
Text Unit 2: Some details about the Test class
```

examples.title: Custom command with regex pattern
examples.description: Defines a custom command using a regex pattern to match application-specific markers in comments.
examples.config:
```yaml
custom_commands:
  - pattern: "###ACCESS_CHECKS###.*?;"
    type: PLACEHOLDER
    inline: true
```

fullDoc:
# Doxygen Filter

The Doxygen Filter extracts [Doxygen](http://www.stack.nl/~dimitri/doxygen/)-style comments from source code for translation. It supports C++-style (`///`), Javadoc-style (`/**`), Qt-style (`/*!`), and Python-style (`'''` or `"""`) comment blocks. Doxygen special commands, HTML commands, and XML commands are recognized and converted to inline codes. The filter preserves line numbers so that source and translated line numbers maintain a one-to-one correspondence.

## Processing Details

- **Encoding detection**: Uses Unicode BOM if present (UTF-8, UTF-16, etc.); otherwise falls back to the default encoding specified when opening the document.
- **Inline codes**: The full set of Doxygen special commands, HTML commands, and XML commands are recognized and interpreted, converting them to inline codes in extracted text units.
- **Line number preservation**: The filter maintains a one-to-one correspondence between source and translated line numbers.

## Parameters

### doxygen_commands

Defines how known Doxygen special commands are handled during extraction. Pre-populated with the full set of Doxygen special commands.

Each entry is keyed by command name (without prefix/suffix, case-sensitive) and supports:
- `type` — `PLACEHOLDER`, `OPENING`, or `CLOSING`
- `inline` — `true` for inline, `false` for block-level (default: `false`)
- `pair` — paired command name (e.g., `code` pairs with `endcode`)
- `translatable` — whether the block content is translatable (default: `true`)
- `parameters` — ordered list of parameter descriptors, each with:
  - `name` — organizational label (not used by filter)
  - `length` — `WORD`, `LINE`, `PHRASE`, or `PARAGRAPH`
  - `required` — affects how aggressively text is parsed as a parameter (default: `true`)
  - `translatable` — whether the parameter is translatable (default: `true`)

> **Note:** The `parameters` listing is optional. When present, parameters must be listed in the order they appear after the command.

> **Note:** Parameters with non-whitespace delimiters (e.g. `.py` in `\code{.py}`) are **not** currently supported.

### html_commands

Defines how HTML commands within Doxygen comments are handled. Pre-populated with the full set of Doxygen HTML commands. Uses the same entry format as `doxygen_commands`.

### custom_commands

Define custom commands using regex patterns to match non-zero-width runs of text within comments. Matches are converted into inline codes. Each entry supports all options from `doxygen_commands` entries except the command name key — instead a `pattern` regex is used.

### preserve_whitespace

Prevents the filter from collapsing whitespace in extracted comment text. By default, the filter collapses single linebreaks within a text run, producing potentially very long single-line paragraphs.

## Limitations

- Single linebreaks in a text run that are not part of a Doxygen command are collapsed into a single long line. No maximum line width is enforced on output.
- Command parameters with non-whitespace delimiters (e.g. `.py` in `\code{.py}`) are not supported.
- Non-translatable command parameters are not exposed for any special processing.

## Examples

### Extracting Doxygen comments

Various Doxygen comment styles are recognized:

```cpp
/*! A test class */
class Test
{
  public:
    /** An enum type.
     * The documentation block cannot be put after the enum!
     */
    enum EnumType
    {
      int EVal1, /**< enum value 1 */
      int EVal2 /**< enum value 2 */
    };
    void member(); //!< a member function.
  protected:
    int value; /*!< an integer value */
};
```

### Inline code conversion

Doxygen commands are converted to inline codes:

```cpp
/*! \class Test class.h "inc/class.h"
 *  \brief This is a test class.
 *
 * Some details about the Test class
 */
```

Produces:
- Text Unit 1: `<1/><2/> This is a test class.`
- Text Unit 2: `Some details about the Test class`

### Custom command with regex

```yaml
custom_commands:
  - pattern: "###ACCESS_CHECKS###.*?;"
    type: PLACEHOLDER
    inline: true
```
